name: Fruit & Fiber Overload Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Grease 12 muffin cups or coat with cooking spray.
3. In a medium-sized mixing bowl, beat the eggs.
4. Stir in buttermilk, molasses, oil, grated apple, and mashed banana.
5. Measure bran, flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and cinnamon into a large mixing bowl.
6. Stir with a fork until mixed, then make a well in the center.
7. Pour in fruit mixture, stirring just until combined.
8. Stir in blueberries or cranberries.
9. Immediately spoon batter into muffin cups.
10. Bake in the center of the 375°F oven until golden and a cake tester inserted into the center of a muffin comes out clean, about 30 minutes.
11. Remove from oven and let stand for 5 minutes.
12. Turn out onto a cooling rack.
13. Serve warm. Store muffins in a sealed bag at room temperature for up to 2 days. For longer storage, refrigerate muffins or preferably freeze.
